“It’s a dream.”

items.title: With Timberwolves selecting Trey Kaufman-Renn at 59 (and Braden Smith to the Pacers at 38), it's just the second time Purdue has had multiple players taken in the same NBA draft.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
With Timberwolves selecting Trey Kaufman-Renn at 59 (and Braden Smith to the Pacers at 38), it's just the second time Purdue has had multiple players taken in the same NBA draft.

First time since JaJuan Johnson & E’Twaun Moore in 2011.

items.title: Braden Smith: It was (obviously) tough for the Pacers when Tyrese, Nembhard, & TJM were all out at the start of the season, and they prefer to play with multiple point guards. Kam’s left-hand dominance and lack of burst/kick were limiting and required a lot of reorienting of the
items.author: Caitlin Cooper
items.body_excerpt: Braden Smith: It was (obviously) tough for the Pacers when Tyrese, Nembhard, & TJM were all out at the start of the season, and they prefer to play with multiple point guards. Kam’s left-hand dominance and lack of burst/kick were limiting and required a lot of reorienting of the offense.

items.title: Braden Smith is basically a better-shooting, worse-defending T.J. McConnell. So, it makes perfect sense that he lands with Indiana!
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Braden Smith is basically a better-shooting, worse-defending T.J. McConnell. So, it makes perfect sense that he lands with Indiana!

I love Smith and think he'll be a high-end backup PG for years in the NBA. He's also an outstanding passer. Pacers teammates will love him for that!

items.title: Pacers make a second-day trade once again. Acquire the 38th pick from Chicago to select Purdue guard Braden Smith.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Pacers make a second-day trade once again. Acquire the 38th pick from Chicago to select Purdue guard Braden Smith.

Few probably know Smith, a Westfield HS grad, as well as Pacers GM Chad Buchanan. They hosted him for a workout on June 15.
